title: Creamy White Sauce Pasta
tagline: One creamy white-sauce base, two ways — classic cream-and-alfredo veg, or a high-protein blended-paneer sauce with seared chicken.
servingsNote: Sauce comfortably coats ~200-220 g dry pasta — don't overload it with more pasta.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients — this is for the classic cream + jarred-alfredo version.

The classic version is rich (~720 kcal/serving). To lighten: use the blended-paneer sauce instead of cream + jar (drops fat by ~15 g and adds ~10 g protein per serving), or cook 180 g pasta instead of 220 g.
Protein boost: the paneer sauce is the built-in high-protein route (150 g paneer). Add a seared chicken breast per serving for another ~45 g protein, or finish with extra grated parmesan.
Golden rule: more pasta does NOT mean more sauce. Pasta water fixes texture, parmesan fixes body, milk only adds volume — reach for them in that order.

items: Salt for the pasta water (season it like the sea)
150 g jarred Veeba (or any) alfredo sauce
20-25 g parmesan, finely grated (recommended for umami)
1 large chicken breast, butterflied (optional, per serving)

steps.title: Boil the pasta
steps.body: Bring a big pot of well-salted water to a boil. Add 200-220 g pasta and cook 1 minute LESS than the packet says. Before draining, scoop out 1-1.5 cups of pasta water and keep it hot. Drain but do NOT rinse; let it steam off 20-30 seconds.

steps.title: Brown the mushrooms
steps.body: Heat 1 tbsp butter in a wide pan on medium-high. Add 150 g mushrooms alone first and let them sit so they release water and brown — don't stir constantly. If they aren't browning, your heat is too low.

steps.title: Add the rest of the veg
steps.body: Add 60 g baby corn and 1/2 sliced bell pepper, season lightly with salt. Add 4-5 cloves chopped garlic and cook 30 seconds only. Lower the heat to medium-low.

steps.title: Build the sauce — Option A (classic)
steps.body: Add 150 ml cream and 150 g jarred alfredo. Stir gently, add plenty of black pepper and a pinch of chilli flakes. Simmer 2-3 minutes only on low — do NOT boil hard or the cream splits.

steps.title: OR build the sauce — Option B (paneer)
steps.body: First blend 150 g paneer with 120-150 ml milk until completely smooth — light-cream consistency, no grains (if grainy, blend more). In the pan, gently cook 1 tbsp butter + 1 tsp olive oil with the garlic (no browning), then pour in the blended paneer and stir constantly on LOW heat. You're stabilising a protein emulsion, not boiling it.

steps.title: Emulsify
steps.body: Add the drained pasta plus 1/2 cup hot pasta water. Toss continuously for 1-2 minutes. If thick, add more pasta water a splash at a time; if thin, keep tossing on low for 30 seconds. It should cling to the strands, not pool.

steps.title: Finish with parmesan
steps.body: Turn the heat OFF. Add 20-30 g grated parmesan and toss fast so it melts smoothly. Taste and adjust salt and black pepper. A tiny squeeze of lemon cuts the richness beautifully.

steps.title: Optional — sear the chicken
steps.body: For the chicken version, pat 1 butterflied breast very dry, season with salt and pepper, and sear in 1 tbsp oil on medium-high for 4-5 minutes without touching. Flip, add 1 tbsp butter + a crushed garlic clove, baste 1-2 minutes, then rest 5 minutes before slicing. Fan the slices over the pasta.

notes: Use less pasta than you think — 150 ml cream + jar + a small parmesan cube tops out at coating ~400-450 g dry pasta, so 200-220 g gives a proper creamy coat for two.
If the sauce looks slightly loose in the pan, that's perfect — it tightens on the plate in 60 seconds. Don't chase thickness.
Malai can replace cream but use less (roughly 120-150 g malai per 250 ml cream), whisk it smooth with 2-3 tbsp warm milk first, and never let it boil hard or it turns grainy.
For freshness, cook the chicken fresh per serving rather than reheating — reheated breast loses 10-15% moisture.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
